regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are currently southwest at 7 knots under clear skies with a 12% rain probability. Expect winds to shift west-southwest at 11 knots later today with isolated rain showers, though the 12% rain chance suggests minimal impact on embarkation or shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Port Everglades and Port Canaveral should prepare for easterly-southeast winds around 10 knots accompanied by thunderstorms and a 28% rain probability, which may affect some outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, a northeast wind of 19 knots is forecast for Miami with drizzle and a 56% rain probability, so cruise operations and shore excursions should anticipate possible wet conditions. Passengers and crew should monitor updates as these conditions develop.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect thunderstorms with southwest winds at 7 knots and less than 10% rain probability across Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, which should not disrupt port operations or shore excursions. Winds are light southwest at 3 knots this morning with overcast skies, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation activities at Key West and the Berry Islands. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds increasing to 12 knots and 20% rain probability with overcast skies will affect Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth, where some outdoor excursions may see intermittent cloud cover but minimal rain impacts. Looking beyond 48 hours, northeast winds strengthening to 16 knots with a 55% chance of rain showers may affect Puerto Plata, La Romana, and Santo Domingo, potentially causing delays or modifications to shore excursions due to wet conditions. Crew and passengers should monitor updates as conditions evolve, especially for ports in the eastern Dominican Republic and northern Bahamas.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds are light from the southeast at 6 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing excellent conditions for port operations and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect easterly winds increasing to around 12 to 13 knots with partly cloudy skies on day one and a slight increase in drizzle and rain probability to 20% on day two, which could impact shore excursions in St. Maarten and Antigua. By day three, winds remain steady from the east-southeast near 11 knots with drizzle continuing and a 10% rain probability, particularly affecting St. Kitts and Antigua. Passengers should plan for generally favorable conditions today, with some light drizzle possible in the eastern ports by midweek but no significant disruptions expected.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing light east-southeast winds at 8 knots with overcast skies and a 16% rain probability, so port operations and shore excursions should proceed with minimal disruption. Passengers and crew should stay prepared for light wet conditions but no significant operational impacts are forecast at this time.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated easterly winds at 23 knots will impact Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with overcast skies across these ports. Rain probabilities remain below 10% in all these locations, so precipitation will not interfere with port activities. Beyond 48 hours, expect a slight decrease in wind speed to 19 knots with drizzle developing in Aruba, which might affect visibility during afternoon shore excursions but with rain probability still less than 10 percent.
